title: dactylorchis sphagnicola
intro: Dactylorchis sphagnicola, also known as the bog keel, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to Europe, specifically found in peat bogs. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, Dactylorchis sphagnicola is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to the lack of extensive scientific research on its safety and efficacy.
